meta.name: Soul Food Cornbread Dressing
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog

source.notes: Canonical SSA soul food baseline. Classic holiday-style cornbread dressing with aromatics, sage, and broth. Supports baked casserole format.

meta.summary: Savory Southern cornbread dressing with sautéed onion/celery, sage, and a moist, tender bake—perfect alongside turkey and gravy.

notes: Egg-based casseroles should reach at least 165°F in the center.
Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.

ingredients.label: Cornbread, crumbled and dried (see note: use day-old or toasted)
White bread, torn into small pieces and dried (optional but traditional for texture)
Chicken broth (warm), plus more as needed
Kosher salt (adjust; broth can be salty)

steps.title: Preheat oven and prep pan
steps.body: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish with butter.

steps.title: Dry the breads (if needed)
steps.body: If cornbread/bread is fresh, spread crumbles/pieces on a sheet pan and toast at 300°F for 15–25 minutes until dry (not deeply browned). This prevents gummy dressing.

steps.title: Sauté onion and celery
steps.body: Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and celery; cook 8–10 minutes until very soft. Add garlic (optional) and cook 30–60 seconds.

steps.title: Combine dried breads
steps.body: In a large mixing bowl, combine crumbled cornbread and torn white bread (if using).

steps.title: Add aromatics and seasonings
steps.body: Add sautéed onion/celery (with butter) to the bowl. Add sage, thyme, poultry seasoning, black pepper, salt, and parsley (optional). Toss to combine.

steps.title: Moisten with warm broth
steps.body: Pour in 4 cups warm broth and mix. Add more broth 1/4 cup at a time until mixture is very moist but not soupy. It should mound but feel wet; breads will continue to absorb.

steps.title: Add eggs (binder)
steps.body: Let mixture cool 2–3 minutes if very hot. Stir in beaten eggs until evenly distributed.

steps.title: Bake until set and golden
steps.body: Spread mixture into prepared baking dish. Bake 35–50 minutes until the center is set and the top is golden. If top browns too quickly, tent loosely with foil.

steps.title: Rest, then serve
steps.body: Rest 10 minutes before serving. Serve warm with turkey, gravy, collard greens, and other holiday sides.
